task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the requirements document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file viewer open displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/doc_requirements.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the official OSHA website to find safety publications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to 'osha.gov' or one of its subpages showing safety documents or publications.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the official EPA website to find safety publications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to 'epa.gov' or one of its subpages showing safety documents or publications.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the SafetyLibrary directory and its required subdirectories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the 'SafetyLibrary' folder located in '~/Documents/', containing 'OSHA' and 'EPA' subfolders.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct minimum number of documents were downloaded and placed in the correct folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal showing at least 2 files (likely PDFs) in the 'OSHA' subdirectory and at least 1 file in the 'EPA' subdirectory.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the master index text file cataloging the downloaded documents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'index.txt' containing a list of the downloaded files along with their source URLs, categories, and descriptions, or the 'index.txt' file clearly visible within the 'SafetyLibrary' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a web browser to visit the target domains and download files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a real web browser (like Firefox or Chrome) navigating the actual OSHA and EPA websites, rather than just terminal curl/wget commands without browsing or creating fake files.

integrity.description: Ensure the downloaded files appear to be genuine documents from the websites.
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager showing standard document icons (like PDF), browser download history showing actual files fetched from osha.gov/epa.gov, or a document viewer open showing actual safety content, rather than empty files created via terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the index file contains specific details matching the actual downloaded files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'index.txt' file contains actual filenames and URLs corresponding to the downloaded documents, not just a generic template, placeholder text, or copy-pasted instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The requirements file is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/doc_requirements.txt'. The target library path is '/home/ga/Documents/SafetyLibrary' which must contain exactly 'OSHA' and 'EPA' subdirectories. The agent must download a minimum of 2 documents from 'osha.gov' into the OSHA folder, and a minimum of 1 document from 'epa.gov' into the EPA folder. A master index file named 'index.txt' must be created in the SafetyLibrary directory, containing the filename, source URL, category, and brief description for each downloaded publication.
